whyItMatters: अब इस लाइव ब्लॉग को रोकने का वक़्त आ गया है. बीबीसी संवाददाता चंदन कुमार जजवाड़े को दीजिए इजाज़त. कल एक नए लाइव ब्लॉग के साथ फिर हाज़िर होंगे. बीबीसी हिन्दी पर मौजूद कुछ अहम ख़बरों को पढ़ने के लिए आप
summaryParagraphs: अब इस लाइव ब्लॉग को रोकने का वक़्त आ गया है. बीबीसी संवाददाता चंदन कुमार जजवाड़े को दीजिए इजाज़त. कल एक नए लाइव ब्लॉग के साथ फिर हाज़िर होंगे. बीबीसी हिन्दी पर मौजूद कुछ अहम ख़बरों को पढ़ने के लिए आप उनके साथ लगे लिंक पर क्लिक कर सकते हैं. - भारत ने इंग्लैंड को हराकर जीता अंडर-19 वर्ल्ड कप, वैभव सूर्यवंशी बने प्लेयर ऑफ़ द मैच और प्लेयर ऑफ़ द टूर्नामेंट - 'मैंने आज जो देखा उस पर यक़ीन करना मुश्किल था', वैभव सूर्यवंशी की तूफ़ानी पारी पर फ़िदा हुए क्रिकेट फ़ैंस और पूर्व क्रिकेटर - इस्लामाबाद में 'आत्मघाती धमाका', कम से कम 31 लोगों की मौत, 160 से ज़्यादा घायल - ट्रंप ने ईरान-अमेरिका की बातचीत में शामिल होने के लिए पाकिस्तान को क्यों भेजा न्योता For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

whyItMatters: A Delhi Police head constable was arrested after his girlfriend allegedly shot herself with his service pistol following an argument triggered by a call from his wife. The 34-year-old woman, who was i
summaryParagraphs: A Delhi Police head constable was arrested after his girlfriend allegedly shot herself with his service pistol following an argument triggered by a call from his wife. The 34-year-old woman, who was in a relationship with the married constable for five years, succumbed to her injuries. Her family alleges the constable lied about his marital status and pressured her. For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Times of India. items.headline: EU eyes broader sanctions on Russian crude
whyItMatters: The European Commission has proposed a broad ban on services supporting Russia's seaborne crude oil exports, aiming to significantly cut Moscow's war funding. This move goes beyond existing sanctions
summaryParagraphs: The European Commission has proposed a broad ban on services supporting Russia's seaborne crude oil exports, aiming to significantly cut Moscow's war funding. This move goes beyond existing sanctions and could render the G7's price cap on Russian crude obsolete, impacting major importers like India and China. The European Commission on Friday proposed a sweeping ban on any services that support ‍Russia's seaborne crude oil exports, going far beyond previous piecemeal EU sanctions in its effort to stunt Moscow's key source of income for its ⁠war on Ukraine. Russia exports over a third of its oil in Western tankers - mostly from Greece, Cyprus and Malta - with the help of Western shipping services. The ban would end that practice, which mostly supplies India and China, and render obsolete a price cap on purchases of Russian crude oil that ‌the Group of ‌Seven Western powers have tried to enforce with mixed success. EU PROPOSES BROADEST CLAMPDOWN ON RUSSIAN CRUDE EXPORTS Reuters was first For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

His action is unusual and unique, with some variations. Ultimately, the decision on its legality rests with the ICC, the umpires and the match referees. Pratyush Raj is a cricket journalist with TOI Sports, specializing in all things cricket, both on and off the field. He began his career with the Business Standard before following his passion as a sports reporter for the Times of India in Chandigarh. Pratyush has also contributed to the sports coverage of India Today Group and his last stint was with The Indian Express. items.headline: Talks with US set to continue after good beginning, Iran's Foreign Minister says
whyItMatters: UPDATED: Feb 7, 2026 06:19 IST Edited By: Nitish K Singh Iran's top diplomat said on Friday that nuclear talks with the US mediated by Oman were off to a “good start” and set to continue,
summaryParagraphs: UPDATED: Feb 7, 2026 06:19 IST Edited By: Nitish K Singh Iran's top diplomat said on Friday that nuclear talks with the US mediated by Oman were off to a “good start” and set to continue, in remarks that could help allay concern that failure to reach a deal might nudge the Middle East closer to war. But Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said after the talks in the Omani capital Muscat, which involved him, U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff and US President Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner, that "any dialogue requires refraining from threats and pressure. (Tehran) only discusses its nuclear issue ... We do not discuss any other issue with the US." While both sides have indicated readiness to revive diplomacy over Tehran's long-running nuclear dispute with the West, Washington wanted to expand the talks to cover Iran's ballistic missiles, support for armed groups around the region and "treatment of their own people," U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Wednesday. A For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.
